name: 2007-2009 Global Financial Crisis
macro_conditions_entering: Case-Shiller peaked Q1 2006 at 198 (base 100 in 1890). Subprime 18-21% of originations (vs <10% in 2001-03). Household debt 127% of disposable income. Top 5 investment banks leveraged 30:1+. Total mortgage debt $10.3T. Private-label MBS doubled 2003-2005.
catalyst_trigger: Cascading counterparty failure from mortgage delinquencies exposing CDO/CDS fragility. Bear Stearns hedge fund collapse Jun 2007 → BNP Paribas freeze Aug 2007 → Bear Stearns sale $2/share Mar 2008 → Lehman bankruptcy $639B assets Sep 15 2008 → AIG $85B bailout → Reserve Primary Fund breaks the buck → $144B money market withdrawals in one day.
timeline_narrative: Housing peaked Q1 2006, Bear hedge funds failed Jun 2007, BNP froze funds Aug 2007, credit markets seized. Bear Stearns sold Mar 2008. Lehman filed Sep 15 2008 (largest bankruptcy in US history). AIG bailed out same week. SPX bottomed Mar 2009 at 666. Recovery required QE1/QE2/QE3 and ZIRP for seven years; Fed balance sheet grew from <$1T to >$4T.
narrative_arc: Textbook Soros reflexive loop: rising prices → looser lending → more buyers → higher prices → securitization demand → even looser lending. Loop reversed: falling prices → delinquencies → CDO losses → credit freeze → forced selling → lower prices. Diversification via securitization had concentrated and hidden risk.
recovery_pattern: Policy-driven recovery requiring unprecedented monetary intervention. QE1 (Nov 2008), QE2 (Nov 2010), Operation Twist (Sep 2011), QE3 (Sep 2012). Housing recovery took 5+ years.
modern_analog_thesis: Elevated CRE leverage, concentrated bank exposure to office (CMBS delinquency 12.3% Jan 2026 vs 2% pre-pandemic), extend-and-pretend obscuring loss recognition, $2.3T CRE loans maturing 2025-2028. Key difference: post-GFC regulatory framework (Dodd-Frank, higher capital requirements, stress testing).

name: 2022 Luna/3AC/FTX Crypto Contagion Cascade
macro_conditions_entering: Crypto market cap peaked >$3T Nov 2021. Terra/Luna UST algorithmic stablecoin attracted $14B in Anchor at 19.5% APY requiring ~$6M/day subsidies. 3AC operated extreme leverage across DeFi/CeFi. FTX valued at $32B with Alameda as affiliated trading desk.
catalyst_trigger: May 7 2022: two large addresses withdraw 375M UST from Anchor; $85M UST→USDC swap on Curve depegs to $0.985. Luna Foundation Guard deploys $1.5B BTC reserves. LUNA supply hyperinflates 343M → 6.53T tokens (1,908,651% dilution). ~$45B Luna/UST destroyed, >$200B broader contagion. 3AC: $3B+ creditor losses, bankruptcy Jun 2022. Celsius freezes Jun 12. FTX: CoinDesk reveals Alameda $5.8B FTT position Nov 2; Binance CZ triggers bank run; $5B+ withdrawn one day; Ch11 Nov 11.
timeline_narrative: Three-wave cascade over 7 months. Wave 1 (May): Luna/UST collapse destroys $45B, triggers liquidation cascade. Wave 2 (Jun-Jul): 3AC, Celsius, Voyager, BlockFi fall as interconnected leverage unwinds. Wave 3 (Nov): FTX/Alameda revealed as fraud, final capitulation. BTC bottomed $15,500.
narrative_arc: Mirrored 2008: interconnected leverage, opaque counterparty exposure, cascading liquidations. Each entity's collapse revealed hidden dependencies. Alameda/FTX was the Lehman moment — trusted counterparty revealed as insolvent.
recovery_pattern: Slow grind followed by ETF catalyst. BTC traded $15K-30K for months before ETF approval narrative drove recovery in late 2023.
modern_analog_thesis: Current cycle has more regulated structures (ETFs, custodial segregation), but memecoin infrastructure (pump.fun 11.9M+ tokens, 98.6% rug-pull behavior per Solidus Labs) creates concentrated retail risk in different form. GENIUS Act stablecoin framework provides guardrails absent in 2022.

name: 1970s Stagflation Cycle
macro_conditions_entering: Post-Bretton Woods monetary disorder: Nixon closed gold window Aug 1971. M2 growth ~15% YoY. Vietnam War fiscal deficits. Wage-price controls creating supply distortions.
catalyst_trigger: Oct 1973 OAPEC oil embargo quadrupled oil from $2.90 to $11.65/barrel. Second shock: Iranian Revolution 1979 tripled prices again. Underlying cause was monetary: excessive M2 growth and persistently negative real rates had embedded inflation expectations before oil shocks.
timeline_narrative: 1974-75: stagflation (recession + double-digit inflation, GDP -1.9%). 1976-79: false recovery, inflation re-accelerated >8%. 1979: Volcker appointed, raised fed funds to 20%. 1980-82: double-dip recession, unemployment peaked 10.4%. Inflation finally broken: >10% → <4% by 1983.
narrative_arc: 60/40 portfolio failed catastrophically — both stocks and bonds declined in real terms simultaneously. Gold was the only major asset class winner. The era demonstrated that monetary policy errors compound through expectation channels.
recovery_pattern: Required extreme monetary tightening (20% fed funds). Double-dip recession was the cost of breaking embedded inflation. Recovery only after credible commitment to price stability.
modern_analog_thesis: Supply-shock inflation from tariff escalation, geopolitical disruption, or energy crises combined with fiscal excess and political pressure on the Fed. 2022 (9.1% CPI, 500bps hiking) was a compressed mini-version. If inflation re-accelerates while growth weakens — exact Burns-era dilemma.

name: 2020 COVID Crash and V-Shaped Recovery
macro_conditions_entering: SPX at ATH, unemployment 3.5%, longest expansion in US history. VIX ~12.5. No elevated leverage signals in financial system.
catalyst_trigger: Exogenous pandemic shock. Fastest bear market in history: SPX fell 33.9% in 33 calendar days (Feb 19 to Mar 23). Circuit breakers triggered 3 times in 8 days. VIX spiked from 12.5 to >80.
timeline_narrative: Feb 19: SPX ATH. Feb 24-28: first major selloff. Mar 9: circuit breaker #1. Mar 12: circuit breaker #2 (-9.5%). Mar 16: circuit breaker #3 (-12%). Mar 23: bottom at 2,237. Fed cut to zero, unlimited QE announced. $5T+ fiscal stimulus across multiple packages. SPX fully recovered by Aug 18 — just 5 months. Ended 2020 up 18.4%. NASDAQ up 43%.
narrative_arc: Both a bottom template (overwhelming policy response = V recovery) and cautionary tale (stimulative excess planted seeds of 2022 inflation crisis and crypto blow-off). Speed of drawdown + policy response magnitude = recovery speed.
recovery_pattern: V-shaped, policy-driven. Unprecedented speed due to unprecedented fiscal/monetary response. Created pandemic housing boom (+40% prices 2020-22), crypto $3T peak, and the 2022 inflation problem.
modern_analog_thesis: Key analog signal: speed of drawdown + policy response = recovery speed. Key divergence: subsequent cycles may not receive equivalent response due to already-elevated debt and inflation constraints.

name: 2017-2018 ICO Bubble (Crypto)
macro_conditions_entering: ERC-20 standard enabled permissionless token creation. BTC ~$1,000, ETH ~$8 entering 2017. Crypto market cap $16B. No regulated derivatives until CME/CBOE BTC futures Dec 2017.
catalyst_trigger: ICO mania: >2,000 ICOs raised >$10B. CryptoKitties congested Ethereum (30K+ pending txs). Retail FOMO drove Coinbase to #1 in App Store. BTC dominance collapsed 85% → 35% — classic late-cycle euphoria signal.
timeline_narrative: BTC peaked $19,783 Dec 17 2017. ETH peaked ~$1,400 Jan 2018. Jan 6 to Feb 6 2018: BTC fell 65%. Total crypto lost >$400B. Dec 2018: BTC bottomed $3,122 (-84.2%). ETH -94%. Recovery took 36 months. >50% public token projects failed by 2020.
narrative_arc: Low-barrier token creation → speculative frenzy → concentration in worthless tokens → regulatory crackdown → multi-year winter. BTC dominance drop to <40% is a reliable late-cycle signal.
recovery_pattern: 36-month grind. Recovery driven by DeFi narrative (2020 summer) and institutional adoption thesis. Previous cycle retail completely washed out.
modern_analog_thesis: 2024-2025 memecoin mania on Solana directly parallels: pump.fun = modern ICO factory, >98% project failure, speculative frenzy in worthless tokens. BTC dominance drop <40% still reliable. Key difference: ETF flows provide structural bid absent in 2017, potentially damping cycle amplitude.

name: 1998 LTCM Crisis (Non-Event Resolution)
macro_conditions_entering: Russian debt default Aug 17 1998. LTCM leveraged 25:1 with $125B notional. Asian financial crisis still reverberating. Credit spreads widening.
catalyst_trigger: Russia defaulted on domestic debt and devalued ruble. LTCM's convergence trades diverged catastrophically. Fed organized $3.6B private-sector bailout.
timeline_narrative: SPX fell 19.3% Jul-Oct 1998. Fed cut rates 3 times in 7 weeks. Markets stabilized rapidly. SPX recovered by Nov, ended 1998 up 26.7%. Crisis resolved WITHOUT broader contagion.
narrative_arc: Conditions resembled systemic crisis setup (extreme leverage, counterparty risk, sovereign default) but resolved quickly through targeted intervention. The 'contagion that wasn't.'
recovery_pattern: Sharp V-recovery after coordinated policy response. Crisis contained to LTCM and direct counterparties.
modern_analog_thesis: Template for when crisis conditions appear but resolve benignly. Key differentiator: concentrated risk in single entity vs systemic distribution.

name: 2011 US Debt Ceiling Crisis (Non-Event)
macro_conditions_entering: S&P downgraded US sovereign debt AAA→AA+ for first time. Debt ceiling brinkmanship. European sovereign crisis intensifying.
catalyst_trigger: Political gridlock on debt ceiling. S&P downgrade Aug 5 2011. VIX spiked to 48.
timeline_narrative: SPX dropped 19.4% Apr-Oct 2011. US downgrade triggered sharp selloff but no recession materialized. Fed launched Operation Twist Sep 2011. Markets recovered by Mar 2012.
narrative_arc: Paradox: US downgrade caused flight TO Treasuries, not away from them. Political crisis produced sharp correction but economy continued expanding.
recovery_pattern: Gradual recovery, policy-assisted. No recession despite correction severity.
modern_analog_thesis: Template for political/institutional crisis producing sharp correction without fundamental deterioration.

name: 2016 Brexit Shock (Non-Event)
macro_conditions_entering: UK referendum on EU membership. Polls tight. Markets priced in Remain victory.
catalyst_trigger: Leave won 51.9% to 48.1%. Markets shocked. GBP fell to 31-year low.
timeline_narrative: SPX dropped 5.3% over 2 trading days. GBP crashed. UK equities fell sharply. Recovery within 2 weeks. SPX made new ATH by Jul 11.
narrative_arc: Market priced in catastrophe that didn't materialize on investable timeframes. Exogenous political shock absorbed within weeks.
recovery_pattern: Rapid absorption. Markets repriced quickly once initial panic subsided.
modern_analog_thesis: Template for political shocks that produce violent short-term reactions but minimal medium-term impact.
